prose: Paraquat is called positive at the assay_mechanism_recovered tier because two independent curated strands converge: CTD Parkinson's DirectEvidence and registration as a stressor in a neuro-AOP pathway [E1]. This convergence anchors the evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis, in which the molecular initiating event is grounded in MitoCarta Complex-I Q-site subunits, with the adverse outcome projected onto the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population [E2]. Brain-penetrance predictions make CNS exposure structurally plausible, supporting the upstream reach of this chain [E5]. Human cohort and meta-analytic data reporting a PD odds ratio near 2–2.5 [E4] are consistent with, though not proof of, the proposed mechanism; none of this establishes causation. The 32 ToxCast assay hits [E3] merely illustrate the postulated pathway and serve as corroboration only—they are anti-diagnostic and never decisive. On this curated mechanistic basis, the positive call is supported while remaining a hypothesis rather than a causal claim.

citations.detail: Curated convergence: CTD Parkinson's DirectEvidence AND registered neuro-AOP stressor.
MIE grounded in MitoCarta Complex-I Q-site subunits; adverse outcome in the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population.
32 mechanistic assay hits illustrate the chain (corroboration only — anti-diagnostic, not a discriminator).
